definition: Treasure Maps are the discoverable navigation overlays within the Liana Banyan platform that guide members through the Trail Head substrate-discoverability architecture. Each Treasure Map is a sequenced path through Trail Heads (Deck Cards = Cue Cards) for a thematic domain — Platform Mechanics, Incentives, Onboarding, etc. The 50 A2 Founder Fire Ledger trigger phrases constitute the seed corpus. QR codes land readers at Trail Head entry points; daisy-chain referential interest produces Bliss navigation (Founder anchor: OH-58 magnesium engine reaction chamber + cyclic/collective rotor control). Maps are NOT static documentation — they are substrate-structural paths that grow as Trail Heads are added. Cephas Proper (Supabase cephas_content_registry) hosts Trail Heads via parent_trail_head_id self-reference.
composing_primitives: Pied Piper of Dragons sharing recursion (B133)

definition: Code Breakers are the member tier/class that earns Glowing Golden Keys through the structured reading-and-quiz pathway within Cephas and the Deck Card system. Code Breakers represent advanced engagement: they read Cephas articles, answer quiz questions (% of page read tracked), and crack the coded layers embedded in Deck Card Frame Locks. The Glowing Golden Keys variant is the Code Breaker-specific reward — distinct from standard Golden Keys earned through ordinary Treasure Hunt engagement. Code Breakers are the upper-engagement tier of the Trail Head / Cue Card stack. The earning path: Cephas article reading + quiz pass + Deck Card keyhole discovery = Code Breaker progression. Code Breakers may unlock higher Recipe tiers (Marks=medium, Joules=rare scarcity) that ordinary readers cannot access. Tier definition is ratified as part of the Deck Card collectible ecosystem.
composing_primitives: Golden Key Treasure Hunt (reading/quiz/% page tracked)
Frame Locks (keyhole levels 1-4 on First Deck Card, higher elsewhere)

founder_voice_quotes: Golden Keys are earned through the Golden Key Treasure Hunt: reading Cephas articles, letters, publications, Pudding source material, answering quiz questions, % of page read tracking, glowing Golden Keys for Codebreakers

definition: Glowing Golden Keys are a Mark-subclass (per BP015 Bishop recommendation confirmed BP015 eblet) earned specifically through the Code Breaker tier pathway — the advanced reading/quiz engagement surface. Standard Golden Keys are earned through Treasure Hunt (Cephas reading + quiz + % page tracked). Glowing Golden Keys are the visual/tier upgrade for Code Breakers who crack deeper Frame Locks. Canonically: Golden Keys are NOT earned by finding Easter Eggs. They are earned through structured substrate-discoverability engagement. The Glowing variant signals Code Breaker achievement tier. Technically: 12 key PNG levels (key-1.png through key-12.png), number in handle of cursor; hover reveals golden key cursor with level number. The glow is a rendering-tier indicator, not a separate asset type. Alternative canonical reading (also valid): Golden Keys are STATUS markers (like Steward Stamps) that unlock specific Cathedral access tiers — non-currency, recognition-class. Both readings coexist per BP015 — Founder call on final taxonomy.
composing_primitives: Mark-subclass (B100 IMMUTABLE Three Currencies canon: Credits / Marks / Joules)
Frame Lock levels 1-4 (HEOHO Museum Card) through higher elsewhere
ratification_chronology: B089 Deck Card system; B100 Three Currencies IMMUTABLE canon; BP015 Trail Head + Golden Keys Mark-subclass recommendation (2026-05-02)

definition: Deck Card Frame Lock thresholds define when and how keyholes on Deck Cards unlock. The HEOHO Museum Card (First Deck Card, B089 origin) has Frame Lock levels: TL=1, TR=2, BL=3, BR=4. Higher Deck Cards have higher lock levels. Lock shapes use NotCents glyphs: ○=Credit (common), □=Mark (medium), △=Joule (rare) — same shapes appear on both Frame Locks and Recipe slots. Recipe slots unlock at specific Knowledge thresholds: cards type-maps to scarcity tiers (Credits=common, Marks=medium, Joules=rare). Scarcity forces choices — the Skittles game analogy (1 red at level 3, pick between recipes). Higher knowledge upgrades card value (VFR→IFR analogy). Empty Recipe slots show greyed-out shapes BEFORE you have all cards — motivation through visible incompleteness. Unlock instructions are unique per card and teach the system inductively: the first unlock teaches what Deck Cards are and do.
composing_primitives: HEOHO Museum Card (B089 First Deck Card)
Frame Lock levels TL=1 TR=2 BL=3 BR=4
ratification_chronology: B089 HEOHO Museum Card; BP005 three-tier paper structure; B100 currency canon (Credit/Mark/Joule scarcity mapping)
founder_voice_quotes: "Each Deck Cards unlock instructions are unique to THAT cards purpose and location"
"Scarcity forces choices (like the Skittles game — 1 red at level 3, pick between recipes)"
"Higher knowledge upgrades card value (VFR to IFR analogy)"
"Recipe slots visible BEFORE you have all cards — empty slots show shapes, greyed-out recipes motivate"

definition: Puzzle and code design patterns in Liana Banyan follow the Recipes-not-spells convention (Founder direct: some people, you know), the Submarine Doors architecture (each card back state = own page/route; back button RELOADS parent in prior state; isolation protects sections; code splits naturally at boundaries — if one section fails others are isolated), and the Multiverse Iteration pattern (multiple state-versions of the same Deck Card back). Deck Cards have UTILITY — they are tools not trophies. Cards slot into Recipe positions for Projects. Bounty Posters ARE Deck Card Cue Cards (shareable via Battery Dispatch). The Cold-Start Crew Bounty pattern: build Steward Cards first; recruit Stewards with Level 1 fill-your-own-slot mechanic; Slot Card shows face + Credits committed = skin in game. All puzzle/code patterns follow the Prove-then-Product principle and the slow blade architecture (do not expose design until complete).
composing_primitives: Deck Card UTILITY model (tools not trophies)
ratification_chronology: B089 Deck Card system; BP005 three-tier paper; B089 Steward system Founder approved
founder_voice_quotes: "Call them RECIPES, never spells (some people, you know)"
"Each card back state = its own page/route (Multiverse Iteration) — Back buttons RELOAD the parent page in its prior state"
"Cards have UTILITY — they are tools, not trophies"

definition: Three-Gear Currency System: (1) CREDITS — $1=1 Credit, One-Way Valve (NEVER cash out to fiat), per-person cap $5,000, used for marketplace transactions, common-tier Deck Card scarcity. (2) MARKS — earned through effort/contribution (NOT purchased), effort-differential currency, one-way ratchet (value only goes up, NEVER down), ALL Marks are backed (80% patent portfolio + purchased credits + earned joules), medium-tier Deck Card scarcity, Golden Keys are a Mark-SUBCLASS. (3) JOULES — surplus/forever-stamp currency, stored value from excess production, rare-tier Deck Card scarcity. Why three currencies dont create arbitrage: each serves distinct function, WIR Bank (Switzerland 1934, 92+ years) proves multi-currency cooperatives work. NotCents glyphs: ○=Credit, □=Mark, △=Joule — appear on Frame Locks AND Recipe slots. SAA (Sponsored Allocation Agreements) use Credits for Bucket stakes. The one-way valve on Credits prevents extraction. The Mark ratchet prevents devaluation. Forman consumption-motive doctrine confirms Credits/Marks/Joules are NOT securities (primary motivation = consumption/use not profit). Path A (SAA only) confirmed. Per-person Credit cap $5,000; per-stake payout cap $10,000,000; SAA influence cap $10,000,000.
composing_primitives: B100 IMMUTABLE Three Currencies canon (Credits / Marks / Joules)
One-Way Valve on Credits (fiat extraction prevention)
Mark backing (80% patent portfolio + purchased credits + earned joules)
SAA (Sponsored Allocation Agreements) as non-security vehicle
Per-person cap $5,000 Credits; payout cap $10M; SAA cap $10M
ratification_chronology: B100 Three Currencies IMMUTABLE; B097 Pedestal/SAA structure; BP005 Forman doctrine; B089 NotCents glyphs; project_mark_backing_oneway.md (B100 confirmed IMMUTABLE)

"All Marks are backed"
"Marks value only goes up, never down"
"Joules: surplus/forever stamp currency — the third gear"

definition: HexIsle is the gaming/maker surface hosted at hexisle.lianabanyan.com (Firebase hosting:hexisle target). It is the sister surface to the2ndsecond.com (DSS/Maker portal). The name Daedalus (Greek craftsman who built the Labyrinth) is RESERVED by Founder for a future HexIsle + the2ndSecond.com architecture primitive — reservation lock as of BP004 turn 9 (2026-04-30). HexIsle geography: island/labyrinth structural resonance (Daedalus brand). The SEVEN-ISLAND geography maps to the platform portal isolation model — Submarine Doors architecture at each island boundary. Each island (1-7) is isolated like submarine door sections. HexIsle connects to: DSS App (platform/src/DSSApp.tsx), Joules as rare-tier currency, Deck Card scarcity mechanics, potential for HexIsle-specific Recipes. Governance: 300 Model with ADAPT Score. Member roles in HexIsle include makers, explorers, guilds. HexIsle is a production system (1 of 36 production systems). PHEROMONE NOTE: HexIsle full mechanics remain significantly under-specified as of BP018 — the most legitimately thin shard in Knight 8. The Daedalus reservation is the anchor canon; full mechanics spec is a pending Founder-ratification item. Phase D note: this entry densifies pheromone from thin→medium only (not thin→dense) pending Founder specification.

definition: Voucher Tiers (also: Vessel Tiers) — 7 canonical tiers per canonical_values.yaml (vessel_tiers: 7). The tier structure maps to currency scarcity: Credits=common/Tier 0-1, Marks=medium/Tier 2-4, Joules=rare/Tier 5-6. Cold-start pathways: 6 canonical (cold_start_pathways: 6 per canonical_values.yaml). Deck Card tier mapping: Credits-type cards are common, Marks-type medium, Joules-type rare — scarcity forces Recipe slot choices. Vouchers are distinct from Membership ($5/year identical for all). Voucher tier progression is not purchased — it is EARNED through platform participation (effort-differential). The Skittles game analogy governs tier competition: at level 3, 1 red Skittle, pick between two Recipes. Higher tiers unlock higher Recipe slots. Tier 6 (Joules) is the rarest and most powerful Recipe enabler. PHEROMONE NOTE: Voucher Tiers Tier 0-6 formal spec is moderately thin — canonical count (7) and scarcity mapping are confirmed; detailed tier mechanics (exact thresholds, unlock conditions per tier) are pending Knight build post-COLOSSUS. The 6 cold-start pathways are an adjacent spec item.

definition: Individual Patent Pedestals are the 10% IP slice per patent asset sold via Upekrithen LLC (Wyoming) as seller-of-record — NOT sold by Liana Banyan Corporation directly. Structure: Upekrithen LLC holds shares in LB Corp which owns IP portfolio. Individual Patent Pedestals (10% slice per asset) SOLD VIA Upekrithen as seller-of-record; proceeds flow back to LB Corp as operating capital (inter-company loan / paid-in capital / expense reimbursement — CPA call). Members who buy Pedestal stakes hold SAA (Sponsored Allocation Agreements) — NOT ownership, NOT royalty rights. SAA = participation rights in allocation decisions. IP Waterfall: Patent Buckets 60%, Founder/Creator 20%, Global Sponsor Pool 10%, Individual Patent Pedestals 10%. Why Upekrithen as seller-of-record: arms-length transaction layer prevents self-dealing; legitimate proceeds flow via commercial mechanism; liability isolation (challenge runs against Upekrithen not LB Corp); QSBS clean; matches Claim D2 IP Load Balancing Scaffold. CRITICAL: Revenue Participation Agreement at retail scale = security. Herjavec-pattern RPA works only because Herjavec is single sophisticated commercial counterparty — does NOT scale to retail. Per-stake payout cap $10M.
composing_primitives: IP Waterfall (Buckets 60% / Founder 20% / Global Sponsor Pool 10% / Pedestals 10%)
Herjavec RPA (50% of Founder 20% slice = 10% total, 20yr, single counterparty only)
ratification_chronology: B097 Pedestal structure confirmed; B126 Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge (#2260); project_upekrithen_seller_of_record.md
founder_voice_quotes: Members who bought Pedestal stakes hold SAA NOT ownership NOT royalty rights
